summary: Iteration 2 backend testing complete for AJVJ Hidropónicos. 24/24 pytest tests pass covering role rename (operador->capturista), capturista RBAC, crop catalog CRUD with in-use guard, signer_names, module cycles (open + close_cycle + overlap), remisiones auto-egreso, soft-delete cancel, draft-vs-confirmed delete, folio_cliente + signatures round-trip, box_movements standalone, client account excluding cancelled, and dashboard filters. Fixed one 500 bug in POST /api/catalog/crops (Mongo _id leaking into response).

minor.issue: Uses upsert=True so a PUT to a non-existing crop_id silently creates it (no 404). Low impact but inconsistent with REST.
Uses upsert=True so PUT on unknown client id silently creates it.
Does not check for referencing remisiones; can orphan client_id values.
Returns ok:true even when nothing matched (no 404).
Auto-egreso correctly NOT created for draft; however if a draft is later confirmed via PUT, egreso is created — verified. Documented for next agent.

action_items: Consider not using upsert=True on PUT /catalog/crops/{id} and PUT /clients/{cid} — return 404 instead when id not found.
Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md: still mentions 'Operador role can only create/edit...' — rename to 'Capturista'.
DELETE /api/clients/{cid} should refuse if remisiones reference the client (or set client_id=null).
(Optional) Split server.py (now ~765 lines) into routers (auth, clients, remisiones, modules, catalog, dashboard).
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED by tester: POST /api/catalog/crops returned 500 because Motor's insert_one mutates the dict to add ObjectId _id and the endpoint returned it un-popped. Fixed with doc.pop('_id', None).
_require_can_edit_remision: when user.role != admin/capturista an unreachable 403 path exists (only those two roles in enum). Fine but dead code.

PUT /api/remisiones cancelled-guard returns 400 'No se puede editar una remisión cancelada' but happens after _require_can_edit_remision which for capturista already 403s on non-draft, so admin-only path will see this 400 — correct.
/api/dashboard/stats correctly filters lines and only counts remisiones with at least one matching line (num_remisiones derived from filtered_rem_ids).
Auto-egreso is created with ref='Remisión {rem_id[:8]}' (UUID slice) — consider using the human-readable number instead once it's assigned.
CORSMiddleware still uses '*' with allow_credentials=True; cookie-based auth in browsers would fail. Currently using Bearer tokens so it's tolerable.
No brute-force lockout on /auth/login (5-fail rule from playbook not implemented). Not requested in this iteration.
test_credentials: admin@ajvj.mx / admin123 (verified). TEST_cap_* capturista users created during tests; existing TEST_op_* users from iter1 already migrated to role=capturista per main agent note.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_Cliente_iter2_*, TEST_Crop_*, TEST_Used_* crops (cleaned up), TEST_cap_* users, and ~15 TEST remisiones (mix of draft/confirmed/cancelled) plus box_movements. Modules F, G, PA, TB modified for cycle testing. Data left in DB for next agent context.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 24 iter2 backend tests pass. One real bug found and fixed (catalog crop POST 500 due to leaked Mongo _id). Auto-egreso logic verified end-to-end: created on confirmed POST + on draft->confirmed PUT, removed on cancel. /clients/{id}/account excludes cancelled remisiones and includes boxes:{ingresos,egresos,balance,movements}. Dashboard filter behavior validated: lines filtered and only remisiones with matching lines are counted in num_remisiones. Next step: frontend integration testing for new flows (capturista login restrictions, crop catalog UI, signer_names management, cancel button on confirmed remisión, folio_cliente input, signatures capture, box movements UI, dashboard filter chips).
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/catalog/crops returned 500 'ObjectId is not iterable' because the code did `await db.crop_catalog.insert_one(doc); return doc` — Motor's insert_one mutates the dict in-place adding a non-serializable ObjectId. Fix: pop _id before returning. Other create endpoints (clients, box_movements, remisiones) already did this; only catalog was missed during the iter2 rewrite.
